title: Elle Language Guide
description: Data types, functions, control flow, and pattern matching in Elle

content.text: Elle supports a rich set of data types. All types have a mutable/immutable split.
**Nil and Booleans**

nil          # the absence of a value
true         # boolean true
false        # boolean false
**Numbers**

42           # integer (64-bit signed)
3.14         # float (64-bit IEEE 754)
-17          # negative
1.5e-3       # scientific notation
**Strings**

"hello"              # immutable string
@"hello"             # mutable string
"line 1\nline 2"     # escape sequences supported
**Symbols and Keywords**

'symbol      # symbol (quoted identifier)
:keyword     # keyword (self-evaluating)
**Lists**

(list 1 2 3)        # create a list
(cons 1 (list 2 3)) # cons a value onto a list
(first (list 1 2))  # get first element
(rest (list 1 2))   # get rest of list
**Arrays**

[1 2 3]             # immutable array literal
@[1 2 3]            # mutable array literal
(get arr 0)         # access by index
(length arr)        # get length
**Structs**

{:x 10 :y 20}       # immutable struct
@{:x 10 :y 20}      # mutable struct
(get s :x)          # access field
(keys s)            # list all keys
**def — Immutable Bindings**

(def x 42)
(def name "Alice")
(def add (fn [a b] (+ a b)))
**var — Mutable Bindings**

(var x 10)
(assign x 20)  # change x to 20
(println x) # 20
**let — Local Bindings**

(let ((x 10)
      (y 20))
  (+ x y))  # 30

# x and y are not accessible outside the let
**let* — Sequential Bindings**

(let* ((x 10)
       (y (+ x 5)))  # y can use x
  (+ x y))  # 25
**fn — Anonymous Functions**

# create a function
(def add (fn [a b] (+ a b)))

# call it
(add 3 4)  # 7
**defn — Named Functions**

# shorthand for (def name (fn [params] body))
(defn greet [name]
  (print "Hello, " name "!")
  (println))

(greet "Alice")
**Closures**

# functions capture their environment
(defn make-adder [n]
  (fn [x] (+ x n)))

(def add-5 (make-adder 5))
(add-5 10)  # 15
**Variadic Functions**

# & rest collects remaining arguments
(defn log [level & msgs]
  (print level ":")
  (each m in msgs (print " " m))
  (println))

(log :info "server" "started" "on" "port" 8080)
**Composition and Pipelines**

# threading macro — pass result through a chain
(-> 5
    (* 2)
    (+ 3)
    (println))  # 13

# threading-last macro
(->> [1 2 3 4 5]
     (filter even?)
     (map (fn [x] (* x x))))
**if — Conditional**

(if (> x 10)
  (print "large")
  (print "small"))
**cond — Multiple Conditions**

(cond
  ((< x 0) (print "negative"))
  ((= x 0) (print "zero"))
  (true    (print "positive")))
**match — Pattern Matching**

(match (list 1 2 3)
  ((list a b c) (+ a b c))
  (_ 0))
**each — Iteration**

# iterate over a collection
(each x in [1 2 3 4 5]
  (println x))
**while and forever**

(var i 0)
(while (< i 5)
  (println i)
  (assign i (+ i 1)))

# forever loops until break
(var n 0)
(forever
  (assign n (+ n 1))
  (when (> n 10) (break)))
**block/break — Early Exit**

(block result
  (each x in [1 2 3 4 5]
    (when (= x 3)
      (break result x))))
# => 3
Elle's `match` expression provides destructuring with wildcards, `& rest`, nested patterns, and struct tag dispatch:

# match on structure
(match [1 2 3]
  ([a b c] (+ a b c))   # binds a=1, b=2, c=3
  (_ 0))                # default

# match on values
(match x
  (0 "zero")
  (1 "one")
  (_ "other"))

# destructuring with & rest
(match [1 2 3 4 5]
  ([first & rest] (print "first:" first "rest:" rest)))
Errors are signals. Use `try`/`catch` or handle them at the fiber level with signal masks:

(try
  (/ 10 0)
  (catch e
    (print "caught:" e)
    (println))
  (finally
    (print "cleanup")))

# protect ensures cleanup runs even on error
(protect
  (risky-operation)
  (cleanup))
Load and compose code with `import-file`. Each module is a closure — imports are parametric:

# in utils.elle
(defn double [x] (* x 2))

# in main.elle
(import-file "utils.elle")
(println (double 21))  # 42
